title: Alectorolophus pampaninii
intro: Alectorolophus pampaninii, also known as Chinese bugbane or Chinese angelica, is a type of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which may aid in treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpenes, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Alectorolophus pampaninii is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support immune function and reduce inflammation.

It is native to China and is also known by its synonyms, which include Ferula pampaninii and Ferula waltonii.
